items.title: 'Finding hidden gems' in 100 years of soil stored at national archive

children.content: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

gallery.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

thumbnail.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

items.title: Rattenbury denies quitting over coalition talks, but they can't have helped

children.content: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a blazer stands outdoors looking serious.

items.title: Educators plead not guilty to assaulting students in Queanbeyan school

children.content: Four educators accused of assaulting children, aged 9 and 10, at a Queanbeyan school were not present in court when their lawyers indicated they would fight the charges.

image.altText: A building with a sign 'Queanbeyan Courthouse'

items.title: 'All you want as a parent': The disability program leading to home ownership

children.content: Not-for-profit Project Independence uses residents' disability support pension to share in a property, giving them legal ownership of their home and allowing them to build equity over time.

gallery.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

items.title: Four metres of WWII 'short snorter' banknotes signify mateship

children.content: It is a crime to deface banknotes, but these rare wartime notes covered in scribbles cause excitement whenever they appear.

image.altText: man in checkered shirt sitting at table with rows of coloured banknotes

items.title: Mystery of WWI digger puzzles NSW community

children.content: The names etched into the Araluen War Memorial are read aloud every Anzac Day, but one digger from World War I remains a mystery.

image.altText: Two women standing in front of a war memorial

items.title: 'Feel it when I breathe': Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program opens

children.content: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

gallery.altText: A man with short dark hair sits on an outdoor bench vaping.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short dark hair sits on an outdoor bench vaping.

items.title: New scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means

children.content: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

image.altText: A person rides a green Lime bike near a fountain in an urban space.

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: 500,000 Australians wait for end to 'limbo' as Butler readies NDIS plan

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

articles.title: Government to pay for showers for at-home aged care recipients

children.content: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

articles.title: Closure of specialist centres for children with autism puts spotlight on NDIS funding

children.content: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

image.altText: A man smiles towards the camera sitting with a young boys in his lap who smiles while looking into the distance.

gallery.altText: Two men in dark suits enter the lower house.

thumbnail.altText: Two men in dark suits enter the lower house.

items.title: In a split second, Keelie made the 'surreal' decision to reveal her abuse

children.content: As Australia marks Sexual Assault Awareness Month, experts and victim-survivors say sexual violence remains pervasive and under-reported.

image.altText: a woman with clasped hands on her lap

items.title: Back of every parent's mind': Experts, junior clubs welcome NRL changes

children.content: Junior rugby league clubs and neuroscientists have welcomed changes to address growing concerns around injuries but say more should be done to protect children.

image.altText: A child stands with one foot on top of a rugby league ball

items.title: Off-the-plan homebuyer 'lives in her car' amid construction delays

children.content: Five years after signing contracts and paying their deposits, families say their lives have been put on hold as they wait to move into their "forever homes" in Newcastle.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

image.altText: Woman leaning on car.

items.title: Fourth man charged over alleged kidnapping murder of Chris Baghsarian

children.content: The 85-year-old's abduction triggered an 11-day search, with his remains later found near a golf course in Pitt Town, in the city's north-west.

image.altText: Chris Baghsarian looking at the camera the man was mistakenly kidnapped

items.title: Gift bags and cruise ship fallout part of Adelaide's COP31 considerations

children.content: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

image.altText: Adelaide River Torrens rowing

items.title: Chocolate-box violin inspires teen to retrace relative's Anzac footsteps

children.content: Ernest Sidney Pilcher died of his wounds in France on June 25, 1918. This Anzac Day, inspired by a remarkable family relic, his great-great-great-nephew will deliver a eulogy at his gravesite.

image.altText: A black and white image of a group of WWI soldiers. One at the back holds a wooden violin.

items.title: 'It's been horrible': Your experiences with family estrangement

children.content: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here is how some Australians have been affected by it.

items.title: Travel quiz: Grab your bags and see if you can beat our quiz

children.content: Which city is hosting the first game of the World Cup? What's the name of Egypt's currency?

items.title: The artworks we can't stop thinking about from the Adelaide Biennial

children.content: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

image.altText: Archie Moore, an Indigenous man, mid-50s, in a cap, holds a heart made of gold in his purple-gloved hands.

items.title: 'Somewhere safe to go': Women's shelter to almost double capacity

children.content: A women's shelter struggling to meet enormous demand says it will soon be able to almost double its capacity.

image.altText: TBA

items.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after election

children.content: More uncounted votes have been discovered in two electorates a month on from the South Australian state election, and while the state's Electoral Commission says the ballots will not harm the "integrity" of the count, they have not said how many have been found.

image.altText: A ballot box during an election.

items.title: Man jailed for mimicking shooting on bridge used in Bondi attack

children.content: A man who yelled antisemitic profanities while pretending to shoot at people from a pedestrian bridge used by the Bondi Beach terror attack gunmen has been sentenced.

image.altText: Bondi Beach bridge three weeks after terrorist attack.

items.title: Young benches ex-coach's son and fires assistant to start Dragons tenure

children.content: Dean Young names the first team of his tenure as Dragons coach, dropping Kyle Flanagan to the bench and sacking assistant and attack coach Michael Ennis.

image.altText: Kyle Flanagan of the Dragons walks off the field, with a teammate following him, both looking upset

items.title: 'I'd still be in jail': The rehab where women find hope after addiction

children.content: Guthrie House is one of the few residential rehabilitation services in NSW designed to help women with current involvement with the criminal justice system get back on track.

thumbnail.altText: A woman stands in a doorway next to a sign that pays tribute to Aboriginal elders.

items.title: Japan to sell lethal arms in move away from post-war pacifism

children.content: Japan has overhauled its decades-long defence export rule, allowing the country to sell lethal weapons internationally amid rising global tensions.

image.altText: A woman speaks into a microphone.

items.title: Sacked civil servant describes pressure to approve Mandelson as UK ambassador to US

children.content: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

image.altText: A man in a black scarf walks sternly.

items.title: El Salvador holds mass trial of alleged gang members for 29,000 murders

children.content: The attorney-general says 486 suspected MS-13 members have faced trial for 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022.

gallery.altText: several rows of inmates dressed in white sit down inside a prison hall

thumbnail.altText: several rows of inmates dressed in white sit down inside a prison hall

items.title: Paramedics unable to help bleeding mother for almost an hour, inquest hears

children.content: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

image.altText: The exterior of the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: Researchers develop surfboard fins to repel sharks after recent attacks

children.content: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

image.altText: Man sanding surfboard in lab, wearing facemask and protective glasses
